summary: A comprehensive implementation-resolved blueprint for an Agent Chat dashboard feature covering backend, frontend, storage, transport, and multi-provider integration. Entity taxonomy is well-organized and decisions are concretely resolved. The file suffers from density problems: its enforces block is extremely long and mixes high-level architectural constraints with pixel-level UI micro-specs (iOS font sizes, composer chip positioning, scroll-to-bottom affordance placement) that belong in a UI-focused companion. The causal when/then chains are well-structured, and the level of edge-case detail (watchdog behavior, reconnect grace periods, queued process-change consumption rules) shows strong live-use evolution. The prescribes block is disproportionately thin compared to the massive enforces block it summarizes.
strengths: Well-organized entity taxonomy with clear semantic groupings (Dashboard, Chat, Provider, Scope, Storage, Transport, Capability, Decision) that make the system topology recoverable from the file alone
Concrete resolved decisions at the correct implementation layer: specific port, package structure, persistence format, provider SDK transports, and cache strategies per provider

Rich evolved behavioral rules around edge cases (watchdog timing, queued message batching, process-completion sentinel states, stream checkpoint recording) that show real implementation feedback rather than speculative design
Strong causal chains connecting UI events through API endpoints to backend persistence and provider lifecycle
Clear V1 scope boundary with explicit deferral list preventing scope creep
weaknesses: The enforces block is a single massive wall of ~120 rules mixing architectural invariants with granular UI layout micro-specs, degrading scanability and making it hard to locate specific concerns

Abstraction levels oscillate between high-level architecture (canonical persistence model, provider adapter boundary) and specific CSS-level concerns (16px font size, chip border positioning, floating overlay inset) within the same section
Some enforces rules read as accumulated bug-fix notes rather than durable blueprint constraints, suggesting the file absorbs implementation feedback without periodic distillation
The prescribes implementation-step block is generic and thin relative to the highly specific enforces rules above it, creating an imbalance where the plan summary undersells the actual specification
Repetition between .means() definition blocks and enforces rules covering the same concern from slightly different angles without clear deduplication
